heroHeadline: US Seizes Iranian Ship Touska; Iran Refuses Islamabad Talks as Ceasefire Expires in 48 Hours
heroSubtitle: The USS Spruance seized the Iranian container ship Touska in the Gulf of Oman on April 20, 2026, blowing a hole in its engine room and boarding it by helicopter — Iran called it 'piracy' and vowed retaliation. Hours later, Iran's Foreign Ministry announced it would not send a delegation to the second round of Islamabad talks despite VP Vance, Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner already arriving in Pakistan. Brent crude surged 4–6% to ~$116/barrel on the compounding shocks. The US-Iran ceasefire expires April 22–23, with no deal in sight. Separately, CBP opened its CAPE portal at 8 a.m. EDT for ~56,500 importers to claim $166 billion in IEEPA tariff refunds ordered by the Supreme Court in February.
footerNote: Data sourced from White House, Federal Register, executive orders, court records, Al Jazeera, CNN, Reuters, AP, NPR, Bloomberg, CNBC, Axios, NBC News, CBS News, Fox News, Gulf News, The National, Newsweek, Nate Silver/Silver Bulletin, YouGov/Economist, Fox News, BLS, Peterson Institute for International Economics, Tax Foundation, CFR, EIA, Treasury, Goldman Sachs, Federal Reserve, OECD, Cato Institute, Penn Wharton Budget Model, IMF.
